metadata.notes:
agent_supports: no
daemon_capability: n/a
driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: no
daemon_capability: n/a
driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

The scenario requires the agent to launch a coding *session* that runs on remote infrastructure with no bound local PID (the Cursor Cloud / Codex Cloud / Devin model) and disappears when the remote run completes. Gemini CLI 0.46.0 ships no such first-party variant â€” unlike codex (`codex cloud exec` runs the task on OpenAI infra) and claudecode (web sessions / routines run on Anthropic infra), there is no Gemini equivalent that moves the session off the local machine.

Evidence the feature is absent:

- `gemini --help` (0.46.0) exposes exactly five subcommands â€” `mcp`, `extensions`, `skills`, `hooks`, `gemma` â€” plus the default interactive/headless launcher. There is no `gemini cloud`, no `--remote`, no `--teleport`, and no deploy-to-cloud / background-agent launch flag. Every execution mode (`-p`/`--prompt` headless, `-i` interactive, `-w`/`--worktree`, `-y`/`--yolo`, `--acp`) runs a LOCAL `node .../bin/gemini` process.
- A keyword scan of the installed bundle (`@google/gemini-cli/bundle/chunk-*.js`) for `CloudRun` / `background agent` / `"remote"` matched only non-feature plumbing: the 45 `CloudRun` hits are google-auth-library GCP environment detection (`isCloudRun()` reads `K_CONFIGURATION` â€” detecting WHERE the process runs, not deploying anything); the `"remote"` hits are OpenTelemetry span-context (`parentSpanContext.isRemote`); the three `background agent` hits are a skill-creator prompt template ("You are a background agent with no user interaction"), not a launch surface.
- The canonical upstream feature request for autonomous background agents â€” google-gemini/gemini-cli issue #4168 ("Run autonomous background Agents", proposing lifecycle management "either locally, by deploying to Cloud Run or through Jules integrations") â€” is CLOSED as not planned. The cloud/Jules background-agent capability the web surface alludes to was never shipped.
- The one genuinely remote feature, A2A **remote subagents** (`/agents` commands, `agent_card_url`), does NOT match the scenario: the orchestrating Gemini session always runs as a local `gemini` process that merely *connects to* and *delegates to* a remote A2A agent as a tool. The user never launches a Gemini coding session that lives on remote infra; the remote A2A endpoint is a delegated service, not an observable session with its own dashboard lifecycle.

Because agent_supports is `no`, the daemon-observation question is moot: daemon_capability: n/a. For completeness on the transport (per the claudecode/codex anchors): even if a Gemini cloud variant existed, the gemini-cli adapter could not observe it as specified. `core/adapters/inbound/agents/geminicli/agent.go` uses `Source: agent.FilesUnderRoot{Dir: ~/.gemini/tmp, Parser: JSONL}` (sessions under `tmp/<project>/chats/*.jsonl`) and matches the process by command line (`CommandPattern{bin/gemini}` via `pgrep -f`) with `DiscoverPID` binding the lowest-PID local launcher. Both axes require a local OS process writing a local transcript; a PID-less, file-less cloud run leaves no trace under the watch. The adapter Source is a sealed sum (FilesUnderRoot / FilesUnderCWD / ProcessOwnedStore per AGENTS.md) with no RemoteAPI/webhook/SSE variant.

driver_capability: ready because the interactive driver is not the blocker â€” the agent simply lacks the feature. No driver step type is missing for a scenario that cannot be elicited; per the frozen-no-feature convention a no-feature cell parks the driver axis at `ready`, not at a `gap`.

## What would flip the verdict

Upstream Gemini CLI would need to ship a hosted/remote-execution product (a Gemini coding session running on Cloud Run / Jules / Google infra with a server-side session feed) â€” issue #4168 reopened and delivered. Only then would agent_supports move off `no`. Observing it would additionally require a new non-file Source variant (RemoteAPI/sidecar) in the adapter model, so daemon_capability would then become `incapable` (matching the claudecode/codex anchors) until such a transport landed.
caveats: A2A remote subagents (`/agents`, agent_card_url) ARE a real shipping feature, but the orchestrating session stays a local gemini process â€” the remote agent is a delegated tool, not a user-launched cloud coding session, so it does not satisfy the scenario's PID-0 remote-session lifecycle.

The bundle's CloudRun strings are google-auth-library GCP env-detection (isCloudRun reads K_CONFIGURATION) and the 'background agent' strings are a skill-creator prompt template â€” neither is a launch/deploy surface; confidence is held at 0.82 (not 0.9) because the upstream cloud-background-agent space is actively moving (issue #4168) and a future release could ship the feature.

If Gemini ever shipped a hosted/remote variant, daemon_capability would become incapable (not n/a): the FilesUnderRoot Source watches only local ~/.gemini/tmp transcripts, so a PID-less, file-less cloud run would leave no trace under the watch.

sources.note: Subcommands are only mcp/extensions/skills/hooks/gemma + default launcher; no cloud/remote/teleport/background-deploy flag â€” every mode runs a local node process

Keyword scan: 45 'CloudRun' = google-auth-library GCP env detection (isCloudRun reads K_CONFIGURATION); 'remote' = OpenTelemetry span context; 3 'background agent' = skill-creator prompt template â€” no launch surface
Canonical feature request 'Run autonomous background Agents' (local / Cloud Run / Jules) is CLOSED as not planned â€” the cloud background-agent capability was never shipped

A2A remote subagents: the CLI 'connects to' and 'delegates to' remote agents; orchestrating session is the local gemini process â€” managed via /agents list|reload|enable|disable, not a remote-session launcher
Source = FilesUnderRoot{~/.gemini/tmp, JSONL} + Process.Match = CommandPattern{bin/gemini} via pgrep -f + DiscoverPID â€” local transcript + local PID only, no remote feed
Adapter Source is a sealed sum (FilesUnderRoot / FilesUnderCWD / ProcessOwnedStore) â€” no RemoteAPI/webhook/SSE variant exists
Contrast anchor: codex DOES ship codex cloud exec (agent=yes/daemon=incapable); gemini-cli ships no equivalent remote-session variant, hence agent=no

recipe.notes: agent_supports=no â€” Gemini CLI has no cloud/remote background-agent variant. No recipe; this cell is frozen.
